patient.query: Yeah, it’s just... it feels like I’m stuck in this loop, you know? I start washing my hands, and then I just can’t stop. It’s like a voice in my head that tells me I have to keep doing it to be safe, or else something bad might happen. But then afterward, I just feel so drained and frustrated with myself for not being able to control it. It's like I'm doing it to protect myself, but then I end up feeling worse.

automatic_thoughts: I have to keep doing it to be safe

llm_response.response: "It sounds like you're dealing with a challenging situation. Can you tell me more about what triggers this compulsion to wash your hands? And have you noticed any other thoughts or feelings that come up when you're in this loop?"

patient.query: Sure… It usually starts when I’m around other people, especially if they’ve been touching things. I just get this overwhelming feeling of panic, like I have to make sure I’m clean. And then there’s this fear that if I don’t do it, something terrible will happen—like I’ll get sick or I’ll somehow hurt someone else. It’s pretty exhausting because even when I know it’s not rational, that fear just takes over, and I feel so helpless. Afterward,

if I don't do it, something terrible will happen

patient.query: Yeah, it really does feel intense when I’m around others. Sometimes it’s just little things—like seeing someone cough or realizing I’ve touched something someone else has touched. It just sets off this wave of anxiety, and I feel this tight knot in my stomach. I wish I could just brush it off, but it's like my mind goes into overdrive, thinking of all the worst-case scenarios. It makes social situations really hard, and I end up feeling so alone in it.

triggers: touching something someone else has touched
automatic_thoughts: thinking of all the worst-case scenarios
